title: Terminal I/O sanitization prevents injection via relay messages
description:
GOOD CHOICE: All relay message text is sanitized before display via strip_unsafe() (ui.rs 136-161). This removes ANSI escape sequences and control characters while preserving printable text and Unicode.

Strip logic:
- Detects ANSI escape (\x1b) and skips until letter
- Removes control chars except \n and \t
- Preserves Unicode (e.g. emoji, accented chars)

A contributor might skip sanitization for "performance" or assume the relay is trustworthy. But a compromised relay could send \x1b[H (cursor home) + \x1b[2J (clear screen) to corrupt the TUI, or send \x07 (bell) to annoy the user.

Sanitization is essential for terminal injection defense.
close_reason: Verified invariant holds in current code; documented in DESIGN.md §14 TUI terminal safety invariants
